steps.title: Condition for Infinitely Many Solutions
steps.content:
For the system to have infinitely many solutions, the two equations must represent the same line.

This requires each of their coefficients (including the constant term) to be proportional by the same factor.

Inspect the coefficients: the second equation 8x + 28y is exactly 4 times the first equation’s 2x + 7y because $ 8 ÷ 2 = 4 $ and $ 28 ÷ 7 = 4 $.

steps.title: Determine the Constant Term
steps.content: Since the entire equation scales by 4, the constant term 9 in the first equation must also scale by 4. Calculating $ 9 × 4 = 36 $, so the value of a must be 36 to make the second equation identical to the first.

steps.title: Final Answer
steps.content: The system becomes identical lines only when a = 36. That matches choice C — C is correct.
